executiveSummary.text: Regime is risk-on but fragile: Nasdaq +0.86% and S&P 500 +0.30% while Dow slipped -0.16%; VIX compressed to 18.92 (-12%) after Friday’s chip-led shock.
Rates remain the gating variable: 10Y Treasury ~4.55% and 30Y ~5.02%, with May CPI due June 10; high long-end yields keep duration/REITs capped even as growth equities bounced.

AI/semis saw a sharp relief rally after the reported $1T chip-stock drawdown: LRCX +7.0%, ASML +6.5%, MU +9.9%, KLAC +9.3%, AMAT +8.6%; this looks like positioning/valuation repair, not thesis damage.

OpenAI reportedly filed confidential IPO paperwork, reinforcing that private AI capex/equity funding demand is moving toward public markets; second-order effect is more scrutiny on AI unit economics and power/compute bottlenecks.
Oil stayed firm (WTI ~$91, Brent ~$94) as Middle East/Hormuz risk persists, but China crude import weakness and OPEC+ quota increases are dampening the impulse.
Singapore income sleeve lagged: banks fell roughly 1.6–2.3% and REITs were soft-to-flat as global long-end yields remain high; no thesis break, but rate sensitivity remains the near-term drag.

regime.label: Risk-on relief rally; macro/rates still the constraint

usGlobal.what: US equities rebounded unevenly, led by chips and Nasdaq; Dow lagged.
usGlobal.why: Post-Friday semiconductor liquidation bounce, Marvell S&P 500 inclusion news and dip-buying offset CPI/rate caution.
usGlobal.implication: Risk appetite is alive, but leadership concentration remains high; breadth confirmation is needed before upgrading regime confidence.

ratesMacro.what: Long-end yields stayed elevated; May CPI is the next macro catalyst.
ratesMacro.why: Strong prior payrolls and oil above $90 keep inflation/rate-cut uncertainty alive; Fed expected to remain cautious.
ratesMacro.implication: If CPI is hot, the relief rally can reverse through higher real yields and lower equity duration multiples; if benign, semis/REITs can extend.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore banks and yield assets softened despite US tech rebound.
asiaSingapore.why: High global long-end rates support bank NIM optics but pressure valuation and property/REIT financing assumptions; local risk tone followed Asia semis volatility.
asiaSingapore.implication: Keep SG dividend sleeve as ballast, but do not add aggressively until yield direction or payout growth improves.

commodities.what: Oil firmed; gold was stable; copper positive; uranium ETF modestly up.
commodities.why: Middle East supply risk supports crude, but China import weakness and OPEC+ quota increases reduce immediate shortage pricing; metals remain tied to AI-grid and liquidity narratives.
commodities.implication: Scarcity sleeve remains strategically valid; tactical adds should wait for pullbacks or clearer real-yield relief.

geopolitics.what: US-Iran/Israel/Hormuz risk remains the market transmission path to watch.
geopolitics.why: The key chain is shipping disruption → oil inflation impulse → higher inflation expectations/nominal yields → equity multiple pressure, not a generic safe-haven story.
geopolitics.implication: Oil above ~$100 sustained would be risk-off for long-duration equities unless accompanied by a growth boom or Fed liquidity backstop.

moves.what: Semicap equipment/EDA-adjacent sleeve snapped back sharply: LRCX +7.0%, ASML +6.5%, KLAC +9.3%, AMAT +8.6%, MU +9.9%; TSM +2.8%.
moves.why: Relief rally after Friday’s chip-led rout reportedly erased about $1T of market value; buyers returned to AI infrastructure/picks-and-shovels ahead of CPI.
moves.implication: Thesis intact: AI capacity still needs foundry, lithography, deposition/etch/process control and memory/HBM. Near-term risk is valuation compression if CPI/yields disappoint.
moves.action: HOLD / opportunistic ADD only on staged pullbacks; invalidator would be hyperscaler capex cuts or order cancellations, not one-day volatility.

moves.what: Shin-Etsu 4063.T fell -6.6%, diverging from US semicap bounce.
moves.why: Likely Asia semiconductor/wafer positioning spillover after the prior US chip shock; no fresh thesis-breaking item found in the 24h scan.
moves.implication: Watch for whether wafer/materials weakness signals inventory/capex softness underneath the AI winners.
moves.action: WATCH; add only if wafer pricing/orders stabilize. Invalidate if management flags structural wafer oversupply or margin reset.

moves.what: QQQ rose +1.56%, crossing the ETF abnormal-move threshold.
moves.why: Nasdaq led on chip rebound and lower VIX, while mega-cap software/hyperscalers were mixed-to-soft.
moves.implication: KL benefits through broad US growth exposure, but the move was concentrated in semis rather than all mega-cap AI.
moves.action: HOLD; avoid chasing index-level strength before CPI.

moves.what: Singapore banks fell: DBS -1.6%, OCBC -2.3%, UOB -2.0%; Singtel -1.9%.
moves.why: Local yield/risk sensitivity and profit-taking; no fresh bank-specific damage found in 24h scan.
moves.implication: Dividend ballast remains useful, but short-term performance can lag when global tech risk-on does not broaden to Singapore.
moves.action: HOLD; add only on yield-adjusted valuation pullbacks or dividend growth confirmation.

thesisImpact.what: AI scarcity thesis survived the Friday/Monday volatility test.
thesisImpact.why: The strongest rebound occurred in bottleneck assets—equipment, memory and process control—rather than generic cyclicals.
thesisImpact.implication: Volatility is the cost of owning scarce AI supply chain assets; position sizing and cash ballast remain key.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD core; use cash only in tranches.

narratives.title: AI goes further public-market funded
narratives.description: OpenAI reportedly filed confidential IPO paperwork. If confirmed through SEC process, public markets may gain a direct AI platform asset, but also impose more transparency on losses, capex commitments and compute contracts.
narratives.priceImpact: Positive for AI ecosystem funding; potentially negative if investors start demanding near-term ROI from capex.

narratives.title: Chip crash or healthy purge?
narratives.description: Friday’s reported SOX/chip rout was followed by a Monday equipment/memory rebound. The market is distinguishing AI bottleneck winners from crowded momentum.
narratives.priceImpact: High volatility; favorable for disciplined accumulators, dangerous for leverage/chasing.

narratives.title: Oil shock without full risk-off, for now
narratives.description: Crude is firm but not disorderly because China import weakness and OPEC+ quotas offset Middle East risk.
narratives.priceImpact: If crude breaks higher, the dominant effect is inflation/yields/multiple pressure, not automatic gold upside.

secondOrderEffects.text: Chip rebound → lower VIX → systematic/vol-control re-risking can support Nasdaq for a few sessions; but CPI shock → higher real yields → reverses the same mechanical flows quickly.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Treat the rally as tradable but not yet regime-secure.

secondOrderEffects.text: OpenAI IPO path → more capital for model scaling → more demand for GPUs, HBM, networking and power; 3rd order: electricity/grid bottlenecks and copper/uranium become earnings constraints, not side themes.
secondOrderEffects.implication: KL’s semis + grid + copper/uranium scarcity barbell remains coherent.

secondOrderEffects.text: Middle East oil risk → higher headline inflation and inflation expectations → Fed caution/long-end pressure → REITs and long-duration tech both face multiple drag, though banks may get NIM support.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Do not view oil risk as simply bullish for commodities; it can hurt the whole portfolio through rates.

investment.thesis: Keep accumulating AI bottleneck assets only on volatility, prioritizing equipment/process control, HBM/memory and grid power over generic AI beta.
investment.trigger: Add in 3-5 tranches on another 8-12% sleeve drawdown or if CPI is benign and orders/capex commentary remains intact.
investment.whyNow: The market just stress-tested crowded AI positioning; bottleneck names rebounded fastest.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, foundry order cancellations, or evidence HBM/equipment supply moves from scarce to oversupplied.

investment.thesis: Maintain Singapore dividend/bank/REIT ballast, but be selective on REIT duration.
investment.trigger: Add REITs only if 10Y yield falls below ~4.25% or spreads widen enough to compensate; add banks on dividend yield spikes with stable credit costs.
investment.whyNow: High long-end yields are suppressing yield assets while cash still earns ~4%.
investment.invalidator: Singapore credit deterioration or sustained refinancing stress.

trading.thesis: Do not chase Monday semicap bounce ahead of CPI; use options/limit orders if adding.
trading.trigger: A hot CPI or 10Y >4.65% likely creates a better entry; benign CPI plus breadth improvement can justify small tactical add.
trading.whyNow: VIX compression after a shock often produces false calm before macro data.
trading.invalidator: Broad market breadth improves and yields fall simultaneously.

watchlist.thesis: Watch WTI/Brent as the inflation transmission trigger.
watchlist.trigger: Brent sustained above $100 or shipping insurance/freight spikes.
watchlist.whyNow: Middle East risk is active, but China/OPEC are currently dampening the move.

watchlist.trigger: Another -5% down day or negative wafer pricing/order commentary.
watchlist.whyNow: Materials weakness can be an early warning that AI winners are masking broader semi-cycle softness.
watchlist.invalidator: Stabilization with US/Japan semis and no margin warning.

riskWatch.name: Hot CPI / higher real yields
riskWatch.description: May CPI is the next catalyst; long-end yields already near 4.55%/5.02% for 10Y/30Y.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash ballast; avoid leverage; add duration-sensitive assets only in tranches.

riskWatch.name: AI capex ROI scrutiny
riskWatch.description: OpenAI IPO path and public AI funding could shift attention from capacity growth to returns on compute.
riskWatch.mitigation: Favor profitable toll-road/bottleneck suppliers over speculative AI application names.

riskWatch.name: Oil/Hormuz escalation
riskWatch.description: Transmission is oil → inflation expectations → Fed/yields → equity multiples and REIT financing costs.
riskWatch.mitigation: Monitor Brent >$100; maintain commodities exposure but do not assume gold hedges every oil shock.

keyEvents.text: May CPI due June 10; likely the key macro test for the relief rally.
OpenAI reportedly filed confidential IPO paperwork.
Chip stocks rebounded after Friday’s reported $1T sector drawdown; Marvell S&P 500 inclusion helped sentiment.
OPEC+ approved July quota increase while Middle East risks keep oil firm.

bottomLine.whatMatters: Whether the chip rebound broadens and survives CPI/rates; whether oil risk feeds inflation expectations; whether AI capex remains funded and profitable enough for public-market scrutiny.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold core AI scarcity assets, keep cash dry powder, avoid chasing one-day semicap strength, and use predefined tranches around CPI/yield moves.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: One-day portfolio noise in SG banks/REITs and generic “geopolitical risk = gold up” narratives that ignore USD, real yields and liquidity.
